title: The Book of the Dragonborn
author: Prior Emelene Madrine
synopsis: A temple prior traces the prophecy of the Dragonborn through the ages, weighing the old verses of the Amulet of Kings against the emperors and heroes who may have fulfilled them.

title: Children of the Sky
author: Anonymous
synopsis: An outsider's attempt to explain the Nords to the rest of Tamriel: their bond with wind and voice, their reverence for the storm, and why they alone were shaped to shout the Thu'um.

title: The Holds of Skyrim
author: Anonymous
synopsis: A brisk traveller's survey of Skyrim's nine holds, their seats and jarls, and the rivalries of climate and temper that divide the frozen north from the milder south.

title: Olaf and the Dragon
author: Adonato Leotelli
synopsis: A bard's retelling of King Olaf One-Eye's legendary capture of the dragon Numinex — a tale the storyteller admits may owe as much to flattery as to fact.

title: The Aetherium Wars
author: Taron Dreth
synopsis: A scholar reconstructs the rise and ruin of the Dwemer Aetherium Forge, and the alliance of four Dwarven cities that shattered in greed over a glowing crystal ore.

title: The Great War
author: Legate Justianus Quintius
synopsis: An Imperial officer's sober account of the war against the Aldmeri Dominion, the sack of the Imperial City, and the bitter peace of the White-Gold Concordat.

title: Nords Arise!
author: Anonymous
synopsis: A firebrand pamphlet calling the sons and daughters of Skyrim to throw off Imperial rule — long on fervour, short on subtlety.

title: The Talos Mistake
author: Leonora Venatus
synopsis: An Imperial apologia arguing that the worship of Talos was never truly divine, offered to justify the concessions made to the Thalmor.

title: 2920, Morning Star, v1
author: Carlovac Townway
series: 2920, The Last Year of the First Era
synopsis: Opens the year-long chronicle of the First Era's final year, as the Second Empire totters and the courts of Morrowind and Cyrodiil circle one another with knives half-drawn.

title: 2920, Sun's Dawn, v2
author: Carlovac Townway
series: 2920, The Last Year of the First Era
synopsis: The intrigues deepen as a poisoner's craft is turned against the powerful, and a young mage is drawn into schemes far larger than himself.

title: 2920, First Seed, v3
author: Carlovac Townway
series: 2920, The Last Year of the First Era
synopsis: Covers the disaster of Emperor Reman III's attempted invasion of Morrowind, and the Dunmer resistance that turned Imperial ambition to ash.

title: 2920, Rain's Hand, v4
author: Carlovac Townway
series: 2920, The Last Year of the First Era
synopsis: Follows the sorceress Turala and the mage Cassyr amid Daedric dealings, as the ruin of a town leaves survivors bargaining with powers better left unnamed.

title: 2920, Second Seed, v5
author: Carlovac Townway
series: 2920, The Last Year of the First Era
synopsis: Recounts the plots against the life of Emperor Reman III and the birth of a daughter whose lineage will trouble the age to come.

title: 2920, Mid Year, v6
author: Carlovac Townway
series: 2920, The Last Year of the First Era
synopsis: Covers the Battle of Ald Marak between Vivec's forces and the army of the Reman Empire, and the strategist's patience that decided it.

title: 2920, Sun's Height, v7
author: Carlovac Townway
series: 2920, The Last Year of the First Era
synopsis: The war grinds on as assassins, generals, and lovers all pursue their separate ends beneath the same doomed banner of empire.

title: 2920, Last Seed, v8
author: Carlovac Townway
series: 2920, The Last Year of the First Era
synopsis: Covers the events leading to Prince Juilek Cyrodiil's assassination and the treaty between Cyrodiil and Morrowind that ended the eighty-year war.

title: 2920, Hearth Fire, v9
author: Carlovac Townway
series: 2920, The Last Year of the First Era
synopsis: Follows the Empress Tavia's despair and end, as the Potentate manoeuvres the throne toward a rule no longer held by any emperor at all.

title: 2920, Frostfall, v10
author: Carlovac Townway
series: 2920, The Last Year of the First Era
synopsis: Traces the mage Cassyr's road to Dwynnen and his fateful meeting with a student of Sotha Sil, as old wrath is finally answered.

title: 2920, Sun's Dusk, v11
author: Carlovac Townway
series: 2920, The Last Year of the First Era
synopsis: The threads of the year draw tight as betrayals ripen and the survivors reckon what the war has cost them.

title: 2920, Evening Star, v12
author: Carlovac Townway
series: 2920, The Last Year of the First Era
synopsis: Closes the chronicle with Emperor Reman III's death, the Potentate assuming rule of the Empire, and the quiet beginning of the Second Era.

title: 16 Accords of Madness, v. VI
author: Anonymous
synopsis: Recounts a duel of wagers between Sheogorath and Hircine, in which the hunt itself becomes the stake and the Madgod proves the cannier gambler.

title: 16 Accords of Madness, v. IX
author: Anonymous
synopsis: Recounts a contest between Sheogorath and Vaermina in which dreams and madness are turned into instruments against one another.

title: 16 Accords of Madness, v. XII
author: Anonymous
synopsis: Recounts Malacath's dealings with Sheogorath, and the humiliating reversal hidden inside the Madgod's seemingly generous bargain.

title: The Wolf Queen, Book One
author: Waughin Jarth
synopsis: Opens the life of Potema, the Wolf Queen of Solitude, whose ambition would set half a continent to war before she was through.

title: The Wolf Queen, Book Two
author: Waughin Jarth
synopsis: The young princess learns the arts of court and cruelty, and begins to grasp how a marriage can be made into a weapon.

title: The Wolf Queen, Book Three
author: Waughin Jarth
synopsis: Potema's schemes reach the imperial family itself, and the seeds of a succession crisis are quietly sown.

title: The Wolf Queen, Book Four
author: Waughin Jarth
synopsis: Open war looms as the Wolf Queen sets her own blood against the throne in the struggle history will call the War of the Red Diamond.

title: The Wolf Queen, Book Five
author: Waughin Jarth
synopsis: The war engulfs the provinces, and Potema learns that even necromancy has a price when armies are raised from the fallen.

title: The Wolf Queen, Book Six
author: Waughin Jarth
synopsis: Fortunes turn against the Wolf Queen as her allies falter and the Empire gathers itself to answer her at last.

title: The Wolf Queen, Book Seven
author: Waughin Jarth
synopsis: Besieged in Solitude, the aged Potema turns to darker arts still, defiant to the last stone of her city.

title: The Wolf Queen, Book Eight
author: Waughin Jarth
synopsis: Closes the saga with the Wolf Queen's fall, and the long shadow her restless spirit would cast over Skyrim for centuries.

title: A Dance in Fire, v1
author: Waughin Jarth
synopsis: Introduces the hapless clerk Decumus Scotti, whose search for work drags him from the safe corridors of Cyrodiil toward the burning frontier of Valenwood.

title: A Dance in Fire, v2
author: Waughin Jarth
synopsis: Scotti falls in with a Khajiit rogue and stumbles ever deeper into a war he barely understands and certainly never wanted.

title: A Dance in Fire, v7
author: Waughin Jarth
synopsis: The final volume brings Scotti's ordeal to its close, having learned rather more about war, and himself, than any clerk should.

title: The Real Barenziah, v1
author: Anonymous
synopsis: The scandalous 'true' life of Queen Barenziah of Mournhold begins with a child-queen orphaned by conquest and hidden away in exile.

title: The Real Barenziah, v2
author: Anonymous
synopsis: The young Barenziah flees her keepers and learns hard lessons on the road in the company of a charming thief.

title: The Real Barenziah, v3
author: Anonymous
synopsis: Barenziah's fortunes rise toward a throne, and the loves and betrayals that will define a queen begin to take their shape.

title: Breathing Water
author: Haderus of Gottlesfont
synopsis: A short treatise, wrapped in a cautionary tale, on the Alteration magic that lets a mage draw breath beneath the waves.

title: Daughter of the Niben
author: Sathyr Longleat
synopsis: A romance of old Cyrodiil that doubles as an argument for the subtle power of Alteration over the cruder schools of magic.

title: Sithis
author: Anonymous
synopsis: A dark meditation on Sithis, the primordial void, framed as scripture for those who serve the Dread Father.

title: Reality & Other Falsehoods
author: Anonymous
synopsis: A mage argues, half in jest and half in earnest, that the laws of the world are merely stubborn opinions an Alterationist may talk them out of.

title: The Lunar Lorkhan
author: Fal Droon
synopsis: A speculative essay proposing that Nirn's two moons are the sundered body of the dead god Lorkhan, wheeling forever overhead.

title: The Doors of Oblivion
author: Seif-ij Hidja
synopsis: An apprentice records his master's dream-journeys through the planes of Oblivion, and the wonders and terrors glimpsed behind each Daedric door.

title: Liminal Bridges
author: Cirantille
synopsis: A technical study of the summoning arts, concerned with the fragile bridges by which a mage draws a bound thing across from Oblivion — and sends it home.

title: The Art of War Magic
author: Zurin Arctus
synopsis: The Battlemage's counsel on wielding Destruction upon the field, where a spell mistimed is a life spent and discipline outweighs raw power.

title: Response to Bero's Speech
author: Malviser
synopsis: A battlemage's sharp rebuttal to a rival, defending the honour and utility of Destruction magic against those who would call it mere brutishness.

title: Horrors of Castle Xyr
author: Baloth-Kul
synopsis: A grim little play in which a Dunmer lord's cruelties, and a summoned horror, spiral toward a fiery reckoning within his castle walls.

title: Before the Ages of Man
author: Aicantar of Shimerene
synopsis: A sweeping account of the Dawn and Merethic eras, when gods walked, mortals were made, and history had not yet learned to keep its dates.

title: The Black Arts On Trial
author: Hanaspia
synopsis: A measured examination of the forbidden schools and the arguments, for and against, that have kept necromancy outlawed among reputable mages.

title: Mystery of Talara, v3
author: Mera Llykith
synopsis: The middle chapter of a romance of lost royalty and disguise, in which a princess must learn to hide in plain sight to survive.

title: Withershins
author: Anonymous
synopsis: A whimsical tale of a priest of Arkay and a very strange sequence of events, offered up as a lesson in the mending arts.

title: The Exodus
author: Waughin Jarth
synopsis: A healer among refugees confronts plague and despair, and finds that Restoration is as much a discipline of the spirit as of the flesh.

title: A Game at Dinner
author: Anonymous
synopsis: An anonymous letter recounts a prince's banquet at which poison is the guest of honour, and every course is a gamble with death.

title: De Rerum Dirennis
author: Vorian Direnni
synopsis: A clever member of the Direnni clan recounts feats of alchemy and enchantment, part family boast and part practical primer.

title: Song of the Alchemists
author: Anonymous
synopsis: A wry poem celebrating the alchemist's endless, hazardous pursuit of the perfect elixir.

title: Enchanter's Primer
author: Sergius Turrianus
synopsis: A plain-spoken beginner's guide to enchanting: how a soul charges a working, and why patience serves the enchanter better than ambition.

title: Catalogue of Weapon Enchantments
author: Yvonne Bienne
synopsis: A reference of common enchantments bound into blades and bows, compiled for the practising armourer-mage.

title: Twin Secrets
author: Vonos Bthar
synopsis: A cryptic fragment hinting at two guarded truths of the enchanter's craft, more riddle than manual.

title: The Armorer's Challenge
author: Mymophonus
synopsis: A contest between rival smiths becomes a meditation on pride, patience, and the difference between a good blade and a great one.

title: Hallgerd's Tale
author: Tavi Dromio
synopsis: A boastful tavern story about a wager, a warrior, and a blade — with a smith's eye for the steel behind the legend.

title: Words and Philosophy
author: Mymophonus
synopsis: A curious little treatise that likens the swing of a great weapon to the turning of an argument, both requiring commitment and follow-through.

title: Battle of Sancre Tor
author: Anonymous
synopsis: An account of the desperate battle at the holy fortress of Sancre Tor, where heavy blades and heavier resolve turned the tide of an age.

title: Mace Etiquette
author: Reven
synopsis: A blunt soldier's advice on the proper use of a mace, delivered with rather more enthusiasm for the subject than most would think healthy.

title: Fire and Darkness
author: Ynir Gorming
synopsis: A study of the Morag Tong and the Dark Brotherhood, and the fine, bloody line between sanctioned killing and murder.

title: The Gold Ribbon of Merit
author: Gorgic Guine
synopsis: A gentle tale of an archery contest and a modest bowman, in which skill quietly outshoots arrogance.

title: The Marksmanship Lesson
author: Various
synopsis: A short lesson on the bow, framed as a hard-won exchange between an impatient student and an unhurried master.

title: Death Blow of Abernanit
author: Anonymous
synopsis: An adventurer's grim account of a ruin, a curse, and a single well-timed guard that stood between the teller and the grave.

title: The Mirror
author: Anonymous
synopsis: A soldier's verse likening a shield to a mirror that shows an enemy his own death, if it is only raised in time.

title: Rislav the Righteous
author: Sinjin
synopsis: The heroic account of King Rislav Larich of Skingrad, who defied an emperor and rode out in heavy harness to defend his people.

title: Chimarvamidium
author: Marobar Sul
synopsis: A Dwemer 'fairy tale' in which a suit of animated armour guards a secret — one of the tales scholars warn are more invention than history.

title: Cherim's Heart of Anequina
author: Livillus Perus
synopsis: A weaver of battle-tapestries recounts the deeds his threads preserve, and the light-footed warriors who danced through the killing fields of Anequina.

title: Ice and Chitin
author: Anonymous
synopsis: A rivalry between two hunters, one clad in ice-forged mail and one in chitin, tests which armour — and which temper — endures.

title: The Legend of Krately House
author: Anonymous
synopsis: A haunted-house tale in which the quietest guest survives the night, and the loudest most certainly does not.

title: Sacred Witness
author: Enric Milnes
synopsis: A study of the Morag Tong's rites, glimpsed by one who crept close enough to witness what he was never meant to see.

title: Advances in Lockpicking
author: Anonymous
synopsis: A thief's practical notes on tumblers, tension, and the patience that separates a locked door from an open one.

title: Surfeit of Thieves
author: Sathyr Longleat
synopsis: A caper in which too many thieves and too few scruples turn a simple heist into a comedy of locks, traps, and double-crosses.

title: Purloined Shadows
author: Waughin Jarth
synopsis: A budding cutpurse learns the delicate art of taking what is not offered, under a teacher with fingers quicker than her conscience.

title: Aevar Stone-Singer
author: Anonymous
synopsis: A Nordic myth of a boy who journeyed among the old gods to recover the stolen gifts of the Skaal, told as scripture in the far north.

title: The Buying Game
author: Ababael Timsar-Dadisun
synopsis: A merchant's shrewd counsel on haggling, in which the one who cares least for the sale is the one who walks away richest.

title: Herbalist's Guide to Skyrim
author: Agneta Falia
synopsis: A field guide to the useful flora of the north, from frost mirriam to snowberries, for the alchemist willing to brave the cold to gather them.

title: The Firmament
author: Ffoulke
synopsis: A primer on the constellations and the birthsigns they govern, and how the wheel of the heavens is said to shape a mortal's fate.

title: Songs of the Return, Vol. 7
author: Anonymous
synopsis: One of the surviving songs of Ysgramor's legendary voyage, praising the deeds of a companion who helped win Skyrim for the Nords.

title: The Legendary Sancre Tor
author: Matera Chevas
synopsis: A treasure-seeker's obsessive account of the lost catacombs of Sancre Tor and the blessed dead said to sleep within them.

title: Kolb & the Dragon
author: Anonymous
synopsis: A children's choose-your-path adventure in which brave Kolb faces a dragon — with the outcome left, page by page, in the young reader's hands.
